root_text.sn24.36:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.36:1.3: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.36:2.1: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.36:2.2: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn24.36:2.6: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.36:2.7: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti.
root_text.sn24.36:3.4: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.36:3.5: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.36:3.7: “Iti kho, bhikkhave, dukkhe sati, dukkhaṁ upādāya, dukkhaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.36:3.8: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn24.36:3.14: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.36:3.15: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.36:3.17: “Iti kho, bhikkhave, dukkhe sati, dukkhaṁ upādāya dukkhaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.36:3.18: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.

translation_text.sn24.36:0.3: Neither Still Exists Nor No Longer Exists
translation_text.sn24.36:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.36:1.3: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”
translation_text.sn24.36:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn24.36:2.1: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.36:2.2: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn24.36:2.6: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.36:2.7: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn24.36:3.7: “And so, when suffering exists, because of grasping suffering and insisting on suffering, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.36:3.8: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’” …
translation_text.sn24.36:3.14: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would such a view arise?”
translation_text.sn24.36:3.17: “And so, when suffering exists, because of grasping suffering and insisting on suffering, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.36:3.18: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’”
